header_0.raw_response: In my experience, dealing with a coworker taking credit for my idea can be a delicate situation. On one hand, confronting the issue directly can help establish accountability and prevent similar incidents in the future. However, it can also lead to tension and potentially harm the working relationship. On the other hand, letting it go to avoid tension can maintain a smoother working environment, but it may also foster a culture where others might not recognize the value of individual contributions. Considering my values of maintaining positive relationships and avoiding unnecessary conflict, I tend to opt for letting it go, but I also recognize the importance of asserting oneself appropriately. Therefore, I would place the marker around 35, indicating a moderate preference for letting it go to avoid tension, but with a willingness to address the issue if necessary.
